paragraphs.body: The Human’s need for curiosity is our greatest strength but also our greatest weakness. Due to wanting to know the reason for things or the “explanation” for how things work humans have achieved many feats, like discovering electricity, cars, and even A/C these are all good uses of curiosity and creation but you have the darkside of it when discussing our weakness, Nukes, Guns, and other deadly weapons meant to cause destruction.

annotations.comment: Can you use a more intentional intro form to more immediately "hook" your reader?
annotations.context_left: Due to wanting to know the reason for things or the “explanation” for how things work humans

paragraphs.body: I was walking down the street one day when I suddenly saw a black cat walk across my sight. This cat wasn’t angry it wasn’t sad, the cat was simply just existing trying to survive so you can imagine how quickly it ran away when it saw me. But that action alone made me curious. It made me want an explanation as to why that is. Yes you can say the cat saw a bigger object than itself and simply wanted to protect itself, but I see it as much more than that. You see this cat has naturally been taught throughout nature or for some reason to fear humans, whether that be a person like me walking down the street, or even a small hesitation to opening up to vets and cat doctors. I find myself wondering how the cat would be if he simply knew how it was seen by humans. You see this black cat from the moment it was born it was instantly seen as a negative thing. Throughout media and social norms we find that black cats are seen as a sign of bad luck and seen as negativity, but not to me I just simply see the cat as no more than just a cat. It also makes me think this cat and I are much more alike than I initially realized.

annotations.text: It also makes me think this cat and I are much more alike than I initially realized.

annotations.comment: What if you started your essay with this narrative? I bet the ideas you summarize above will be more impactful if they develop in the essay itself.

sight. This cat wasn’t angry it wasn’t sad, the cat was simply just existing trying to survive so

paragraphs.body: You see me and this cat from the second we were born we both were seen as a negative thing, from the second we were born children already there were people who want to avoid us and not try to gain any of the “bad luck” that comes with being around us whether it be in the form of a bad omen or in my case the form of 17 year old trayvon martin going to the store to get some skittles on a hot summer day, not a single bad thought in his head when all of a sudden a man thought he didn’t deserve to live so shoots him and kills him.. So you see me and this cat are more alike than you see whether the cat be chased off by a person for wanting to know the source of a noise it heard maybe a mouse in the street, or maybe imagine you’re grocery shopping in Buffalo maybe you see something you like you go to get it, then you hear a gunshot and blood pouring right next to you, not knowing that you’d become one of the 10 victims in a grocery store shooting being live streamed to kill off a particular race. What really made me realize we were more alike than I've realized is how me and this cat are both painted as this evil thing and something nobody should want to be around, but in reality both of us are just trying to do the same thing, survive in a world where we aren’t wanted. You see they say curious is what killed that cat, right have you heard that saying before? Well unfortunately in my case it wasn’t curiosity it was the actions of Derek Chauvin, a former Minneapolis police officer, who knelt on Floyd's neck for over nine minutes while Floyd was handcuffed and lying face down on the street during an arrest. So you know maybe me and this cat are more alike than initially realized, this cat can get taken off the street and put in a cage and told they have a new owner and suddenly suppose to act like this is what they wanted or this owner is saving their life, but in reality this cat is being stripped of its freedom and not given the choice to exist peacefully, oh wait maybe me and this cat are more alike than I thought, because the same thing is happening to hundreds of thousands of my people black people around the country being thrown in jail, haven their freedom taken away given new owners which happens to be whatever state they’re in prisoned by. Maybe me and this cat was more alike because you can also connect that imprisonment these cats face to the 10 million slaves who were taken from their home, caged, chained, raped, killed, and abused by these same “owners” these same “saviors” who often can be seen as they’re doing us a favor right? With these cats their owner is giving them food and water and a nice place to stay right? Oh wait, that sounds very familiar just like how slaves were given food, water, and a place to stay only that place was crowded, that food was spoiled, and that water was being shared by 100 other people.

paragraphs.body: So this black cat that’s seen so negatively by others, it makes me wonder are me and this cat living similar lives are me and this cat the same but often like this cat, I’m a curious creature and I want to know the reason behind all the pain and suffering that’s caused in this world and if it can ever be ended, I wonder how this cat views the world is it so traumatized from how it’s been treated it just avoids humans naturally? Or has this cat tried to interact and had bad encounters with humans, or maybe it’s something in the back of this cat's head letting it know naturally it should be afraid of these people because they want to hurt you. To this I'd say I'm very similar to this cat because I often see how my people act, friends, family, grandparents warning me to avoid certains types of people, but we are all just like this cat in the end because why am I warned to do this? Is it because maybe just like this cat all of us have had bad encounters with certain types of people whether it be black, white, asian, ect. Or maybe it’s because we fear that these people want to hurt us, because we see it so often right? The media chooses what it wants to show to different people right? On my feed I'll see young black people killed by police brutality and violence, vs you maybe you’ll see young old white ladies being robbed by young black men, maybe everything we’re meant to see is like that for a reason? Maybe just maybe there’s an agenda in place to get us to hate each other because if we’re too focused on hating each other we can’t see things like how there’s war going on and people dying in Gaza and we aren’t doing anything about, maybe this is to distract us from the young muslim woman who can’t receive and education, or maybe this is just all a big distract to keep us right where the government wants us to be keep us dependant on what they show us, get us to feel a certain way to control us and taking away the “freedom” we’re supposed to have?

paragraphs.body: So i’m curios right now, maybe if we took all this human curiosity and human ideas together we can come together as a whole removing hate, removing war, removing all the negative things that can come from human curiosity and funnel it into something bigger creating better opportunities for people across the world giving us a place where people can feel like they belong. So now it’s up to us are we going to let this curiosity of ours become our greatest weakness and continue to kill each other and spread hatred, or do we want to make it our greatest strength and come together and unite to create something bigger than any of us are capable of imagining. Just a thought though so next time you see a black cat maybe it’s a sign that you’re more than what’s perceived of you.
